steps.title: Fastest path: mark the relationships first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find angle T using the triangle angle sum
steps.content:
The three angles of triangle $RST$ sum to $180^{\circ}$. Setting up the equation:
$$45 + x + (4x - 5) = 180$$
Combining like terms:
$$40 + 5x = 180$$
Solving for $x$:
$$5x = 140$$
$$x = 28$$
Now we can find angle $T$:
$$\text{Angle } T = 4x - 5 = 4(28) - 5 = 112 - 5 = 107^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Apply corresponding angles with parallel lines
steps.content:
Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, with $\overline{ST}$ as a transversal cutting the two parallel lines, angle $SKL$ and angle $STR$ are corresponding angles.

When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, corresponding angles are equal.

Therefore, angle $SKL$ = angle $T$ = 107 degrees.
